facility_name: University of Munster CIT -- ZutaCore HyperCool Waterless DLC
opportunity_summary: University HPC data center retrofitted with ZutaCore HyperCool waterless two-phase DLC. Eliminated water leakage risk, reduced energy costs significantly. Plans to expand HyperCool to all future HPC needs. Small academic DC, ideal Solar Steam prospect.
notes: Confirmed ZutaCore customer. CIT (Center for Information Technology). Limited data center real estate driving high-density liquid cooling adoption. Under 2MW estimated.

facility_name: Adacen / DUG Nomad -- Silver Spring Immersion Cooling Demo
opportunity_summary: Adacen's 12401 Prosperity Drive facility hosts DUG Nomad prototype immersion cooling container. 214,000 sq ft facility with 6x 2.4MW generators. DUG Nomad units available in 10/20/40-foot containers. Demonstration site for customer evaluations.
notes: Active demo site since Oct 2024. Adacen HQ in Albuquerque NM. DUG Nomad 10: 26RU immersed, 40kW heat rejection, 150kW 3-phase power per pod. Sansone Group acquired building.

facility_name: Asperitas / UNICOM Engineering -- DFCX Immersion Lab (Plano, TX)
opportunity_summary: Asperitas DFCX immersion cooling platform deployed in UNICOM's Plano TX lab to validate immersion-ready server configurations under production conditions. Single-contract path to immersion-cooled infrastructure. Edge, cloud, and HPC support.
notes: Announced Feb 2026. Asperitas also partners with STECON GROUP for Southeast Asia. Participates in European 8ra IPCEI initiative.

facility_name: Colovore SJC02 -- Liquid-Cooled AI Colocation (Santa Clara)
cooling_type: direct liquid cooling + rear door heat exchangers
opportunity_summary: Pioneer liquid-cooled colocation in Silicon Valley. SJC02 is 9MW, supports 17kW-500kW+ per rack via DLC and RDHx. NVIDIA DGX Ready certified. Customers include Cerebras, Lambda Cloud, Cirrascale. Acquired by King Street for rapid scaling.
notes: Launched 2013, SJC01 at 1101 Space Park Drive. SJC02 announced Nov 2022 on neighboring plot. Acquired 2025 by King Street Capital. Planning expansion to additional Tier 1 US markets.

facility_name: Osaka University Cybermedia Center -- OCTOPUS (Asetek DLC)
cooling_type: direct-to-chip liquid cooling (Asetek RackCDU D2C + InRackCDU)
opportunity_summary: 1.463 PFLOPS hybrid HPC system with Asetek hot water DLC on 236 Skylake nodes + 44 KNL nodes + 37 P100 GPU nodes. RackCDU captures 60-80% of server heat, cutting cooling costs 50%. University campus DC under 5MW. Established liquid cooling reference.
notes: OCTOPUS operational since 2017. Asetek InRackCDU technology. Hybrid cluster: CPU, many-core, GPU, large memory, and storage. Campus data center scale ideal for Solar Steam.

facility_name: NERSC Cech Early Access System -- Rittal DLC (Berkeley Lab)
cooling_type: direct liquid cooling (Dell ORv3 + Rittal V3.5 CDU)
opportunity_summary: Early access system for NERSC-10 Doudna supercomputer. Dell IR7000 direct liquid-cooled rack + first US deployment of Rittal V3.5 CDU. NVIDIA Vera-Rubin platform pending. Cech delivered Jan 2026, full Doudna late 2026. National lab small-scale HPC.
notes: Cech is pilot partition for Doudna. Full Doudna will be larger but Cech is ~2MW DLC testbed. First US Rittal V3.5 CDU deployment. Berkeley Lab campus environment.

facility_name: Vertiv CoolCenter Immersion -- Digital Realty ROM1 (Rome, Italy)
opportunity_summary: Digital Realty's first Italian DC. 3MW+ initial capacity. Vertiv CoolCenter Immersion (launched Nov 2025) for AI/HPC. Free-cooling leveraging Rome climate. Carrier-neutral, optimized for AI/ML. Mediterranean connectivity hub. Expected live 2027.
notes: Phase-based deployment. Rome's climate supports free cooling optimization. Connects to Marseille, Athens, Crete. Barcelona expansion launching early 2026. ROM1 is small initial phase.

facility_name: Nscale Glomfjord -- AI Data Center (Direct-to-Chip DLC)
opportunity_summary: Nscale AI hyperscaler expanding existing 30MW facility to 60MW in Glomfjord, Norway. Arctic location. Closed-loop DLC drastically reducing water use. Initial 10MW phase target. Q2 2025 go-live. Renewable hydro/wind power. Sovereign European AI compute.
notes: Nscale also building 50-90MW in Loughton UK (Q4 2026) and planning $2.5B UK investment. Glomfjord expansion from 30MW existing to 60MW total. Arctic climate provides natural cooling advantage.

facility_name: Asperitas / STECON GROUP -- Southeast Asia Green DC (Planned)
opportunity_summary: Asperitas and STECON GROUP partnership to accelerate Southeast Asia's green DC future. Immersion cooling critical in tropical climates where air cooling is least effective. Thailand/SEA region rapidly building AI infrastructure. High Solar Steam potential.
notes: Partnership announced Nov 2025. STECON is major Thai infrastructure company. Specific site TBD but partnership targets SEA market. Tropical climate = high cooling demand = strong Solar Steam fit.

facility_name: CoolIT Starfield -- Manufacturing + Demo Facility (Calgary, Canada)
cooling_type: direct-to-chip liquid cooling (CoolIT OMNI cold plates + CDUs)
opportunity_summary: CoolIT's Starfield facility in Calgary: manufacturing hub + Liquid Lab Innovation Center for customer demos and testing. 25X production scaling in 18 months. 2MW CDU launched April 2025. 300+ DC deployments worldwide. Acquired by Ecolab for $4.75B (March 2026).
notes: CoolIT is DLC market leader by volume. OMNI cold plate optimized for NVIDIA GB200/GB300. Starfield opened 2024. Ecolab acquisition pending. Canadian HQ = Solar Steam home market advantage.

facility_name: Iceotope Labs -- Precision Liquid Cooling Innovation Center (Sheffield, UK)
opportunity_summary: Industry-first Precision Liquid Cooling lab at Iceotope HQ. Provides quantitative data for customer deployment decisions. Partnered with Efficiency IT, Juniper Networks, Engineered Fluids, Meta. KUL AI server: Gigabyte G293 + Intel Xeon. CRN Top 50 2026.
notes: Lab launched July 2024. Meta storage liquid cooling study proved 3C temp variation across 72 HDDs. Juniper partnership for AI switching fabric + immersion. Global HQ in Sheffield.

facility_name: nVent SCHROFF LFT -- Military Tactical Edge (VITA 48.4)
opportunity_summary: nVent SCHROFF LFT module: VITA 48.4 standard, 300W/6U cooling, leak-proof quick-disconnect. Purpose-built for tactical edge: AI signal processing, radar, electronic warfare, HPC. SWaP-constrained defense systems. New Blaine MN factory early 2026.
notes: Military/defense edge is distinct market segment. VITA 48.4 is military standard for liquid flow-through cooling. nVent also supplies commercial DC cooling (row/rack CDUs launching H1 2026). Siemens partnership for 100MW reference architecture.

facility_name: Submer MegaPod -- Modular DC Container (800kW per 40ft)
cooling_type: single-phase immersion (SmartPodXL/XL+, up to 800kW per 40ft container)
opportunity_summary: Submer MegaPod: SmartPodXL + SmartPodXL+ technology, up to 800kW compute in 40ft modular footprint. Turnkey solution via modular DC partners. Fully operational in under 16 weeks. 400MW+ total liquid-cooled deployments globally. SmartPod EVO: 140kW per pod.
notes: Submer surpassed 400MW in liquid-cooled deployments. Inspur collaboration for 100kW+ Chinese hyperscale racks. Madhya Pradesh India MoU for 1GW. Individual MegaPod units are small-scale targets.

opportunity_summary: LiquidStack GigaModular: modular immersion container up to 1.5MW per unit. Quoting orders began mid-Q1 2026. DataTank technology proven at NTT (Japan) and Microsoft (Project Natick follow-on). Trane Technologies acquired LiquidStack Feb 2026. Pre-fab immersion at scale.
notes: Trane Technologies acquisition announced Feb 2026. GigaModular is production-ready modular immersion. LiquidStack heritage: Allied Control/Bitfury (160MW+ deployed historically). NTT Japan and Microsoft are proven customers.
